     184              : /*
     185              :  * get_ordering_op_properties
     186              :  *              Given the OID of an ordering operator (a btree "<" or ">" operator),
     187              :  *              determine its opfamily, its declared input datatype, and its
     188              :  *              strategy number (BTLessStrategyNumber or BTGreaterStrategyNumber).
     189              :  *
     190              :  * Returns true if successful, false if no matching pg_amop entry exists.
     191              :  * (This indicates that the operator is not a valid ordering operator.)
     192              :  *
     193              :  * Note: the operator could be registered in multiple families, for example
     194              :  * if someone were to build a "reverse sort" opfamily.  This would result in
     195              :  * uncertainty as to whether "ORDER BY USING op" would default to NULLS FIRST
     196              :  * or NULLS LAST, as well as inefficient planning due to failure to match up
     197              :  * pathkeys that should be the same.  So we want a determinate result here.
     198              :  * Because of the way the syscache search works, we'll use the interpretation
     199              :  * associated with the opfamily with smallest OID, which is probably
     200              :  * determinate enough.  Since there is no longer any particularly good reason
     201              :  * to build reverse-sort opfamilies, it doesn't seem worth expending any
     202              :  * additional effort on ensuring consistency.
     203              :  */
     204              : bool
     205          476 : get_ordering_op_properties(Oid opno,
     206              :                                                    Oid *opfamily, Oid *opcintype, int16 *strategy)
     207              : {
     208          476 :         bool            result = false;
     209              :         CatCList   *catlist;
     210              :         int                     i;
     211              : 
     212              :         /* ensure outputs are initialized on failure */
     213          476 :         *opfamily = InvalidOid;
     214          476 :         *opcintype = InvalidOid;
     215          476 :         *strategy = 0;
     216              : 
     217              :         /*
     218              :          * Search pg_amop to see if the target operator is registered as the "<"
     219              :          * or ">" operator of any btree opfamily.
     220              :          */
     221          476 :         catlist = SearchSysCacheList1(AMOPOPID, ObjectIdGetDatum(opno));
     222              : 
     223          476 :         for (i = 0; i < catlist->n_members; i++)
     224              :         {
     225          476 :                 HeapTuple       tuple = &catlist->members[i]->tuple;
     226          476 :                 Form_pg_amop aform = (Form_pg_amop) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
     227              : 
     228              :                 /* must be btree */
     229          476 :                 if (aform->amopmethod != BTREE_AM_OID)
     230            0 :                         continue;
     231              : 
     232          476 :                 if (aform->amopstrategy == BTLessStrategyNumber ||
     233            2 :                         aform->amopstrategy == BTGreaterStrategyNumber)
     234              :                 {
     235              :                         /* Found it ... should have consistent input types */
     236          476 :                         if (aform->amoplefttype == aform->amoprighttype)
     237              :                         {
     238              :                                 /* Found a suitable opfamily, return info */
     239          476 :                                 *opfamily = aform->amopfamily;
     240          476 :                                 *opcintype = aform->amoplefttype;
     241          476 :                                 *strategy = aform->amopstrategy;
     242          476 :                                 result = true;
     243          476 :                                 break;
     244              :                         }
     245              :                 }
     246              :         }
     247              : 
     248          476 :         ReleaseSysCacheList(catlist);
     249              : 
     250          476 :         return result;
     251              : }
     252              : 
     253              : /*
     254              :  * get_equality_op_for_ordering_op
     255              :  *              Get the OID of the datatype-specific btree equality operator
     256              :  *              associated with an ordering operator (a "<" or ">" operator).
     257              :  *
     258              :  * If "reverse" isn't NULL, also set *reverse to false if the operator is "<",
     259              :  * true if it's ">"
     260              :  *
     261              :  * Returns InvalidOid if no matching equality operator can be found.
     262              :  * (This indicates that the operator is not a valid ordering operator.)
     263              :  */
     264              : Oid
     265            0 : get_equality_op_for_ordering_op(Oid opno, bool *reverse)

     274              :                                                                    &opfamily, &opcintype, &strategy))
     275              :         {
     276              :                 /* Found a suitable opfamily, get matching equality operator */
     277            0 :                 result = get_opfamily_member(opfamily,
     278              :                                                                          opcintype,
     279              :                                                                          opcintype,
     280              :                                                                          BTEqualStrategyNumber);

     282            0 :                         *reverse = (strategy == BTGreaterStrategyNumber);

     284              : 
     285            0 :         return result;

     288              : /*
     289              :  * get_ordering_op_for_equality_op
     290              :  *              Get the OID of a datatype-specific btree ordering operator
     291              :  *              associated with an equality operator.  (If there are multiple
     292              :  *              possibilities, assume any one will do.)
     293              :  *
     294              :  * This function is used when we have to sort data before unique-ifying,
     295              :  * and don't much care which sorting op is used as long as it's compatible
     296              :  * with the intended equality operator.  Since we need a sorting operator,
     297              :  * it should be single-data-type even if the given operator is cross-type.
     298              :  * The caller specifies whether to find an op for the LHS or RHS data type.
     299              :  *
     300              :  * Returns InvalidOid if no matching ordering operator can be found.
     301              :  */
     302              : Oid

     307              :         int                     i;

     309              :         /*
     310              :          * Search pg_amop to see if the target operator is registered as the "="
     311              :          * operator of any btree opfamily.
     312              :          */

     317            0 :                 HeapTuple       tuple = &catlist->members[i]->tuple;

     321            0 :                 if (aform->amopmethod != BTREE_AM_OID)
     322            0 :                         continue;
     323              : 
     324            0 :                 if (aform->amopstrategy == BTEqualStrategyNumber)
     325              :                 {
     326              :                         /* Found a suitable opfamily, get matching ordering operator */
     327              :                         Oid                     typid;
     328              : 
     329            0 :                         typid = use_lhs_type ? aform->amoplefttype : aform->amoprighttype;
     330            0 :                         result = get_opfamily_member(aform->amopfamily,
     331              :                                                                                  typid, typid,
     332              :                                                                                  BTLessStrategyNumber);
     333            0 :                         if (OidIsValid(result))

     335              :                         /* failure probably shouldn't happen, but keep looking if so */

     343              : 
     344              : /*
     345              :  * get_mergejoin_opfamilies
     346              :  *              Given a putatively mergejoinable operator, return a list of the OIDs
     347              :  *              of the btree opfamilies in which it represents equality.
     348              :  *
     349              :  * It is possible (though at present unusual) for an operator to be equality
     350              :  * in more than one opfamily, hence the result is a list.  This also lets us
     351              :  * return NIL if the operator is not found in any opfamilies.
     352              :  *
     353              :  * The planner currently uses simple equal() tests to compare the lists
     354              :  * returned by this function, which makes the list order relevant, though
     355              :  * strictly speaking it should not be.  Because of the way syscache list
     356              :  * searches are handled, in normal operation the result will be sorted by OID
     357              :  * so everything works fine.  If running with system index usage disabled,
     358              :  * the result ordering is unspecified and hence the planner might fail to
     359              :  * recognize optimization opportunities ... but that's hardly a scenario in
     360              :  * which performance is good anyway, so there's no point in expending code
     361              :  * or cycles here to guarantee the ordering in that case.
     362              :  */
     363              : List *
     364          668 : get_mergejoin_opfamilies(Oid opno)
     365              : {
     366          668 :         List       *result = NIL;
     367              :         CatCList   *catlist;
     368              :         int                     i;
     369              : 
     370              :         /*
     371              :          * Search pg_amop to see if the target operator is registered as the "="
     372              :          * operator of any btree opfamily.
     373              :          */
     374          668 :         catlist = SearchSysCacheList1(AMOPOPID, ObjectIdGetDatum(opno));
     375              : 
     376         2667 :         for (i = 0; i < catlist->n_members; i++)
     377              :         {
     378         1999 :                 HeapTuple       tuple = &catlist->members[i]->tuple;
     379         1999 :                 Form_pg_amop aform = (Form_pg_amop) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
     380              : 
     381              :                 /* must be btree equality */
     382         1999 :                 if (aform->amopmethod == BTREE_AM_OID &&
     383          669 :                         aform->amopstrategy == BTEqualStrategyNumber)
     384          669 :                         result = lappend_oid(result, aform->amopfamily);
     385              :         }
     386              : 
     387          668 :         ReleaseSysCacheList(catlist);
     388              : 
     389          668 :         return result;
     390              : }

     392              : /*
     393              :  * get_compatible_hash_operators
     394              :  *              Get the OID(s) of hash equality operator(s) compatible with the given
     395              :  *              operator, but operating on its LHS and/or RHS datatype.
     396              :  *
     397              :  * An operator for the LHS type is sought and returned into *lhs_opno if
     398              :  * lhs_opno isn't NULL.  Similarly, an operator for the RHS type is sought
     399              :  * and returned into *rhs_opno if rhs_opno isn't NULL.
     400              :  *
     401              :  * If the given operator is not cross-type, the results should be the same
     402              :  * operator, but in cross-type situations they will be different.
     403              :  *
     404              :  * Returns true if able to find the requested operator(s), false if not.
     405              :  * (This indicates that the operator should not have been marked oprcanhash.)
     406              :  */
     407              : bool
     408            2 : get_compatible_hash_operators(Oid opno,
     409              :                                                           Oid *lhs_opno, Oid *rhs_opno)

     425              :          */

     492              : /*
     493              :  * get_op_hash_functions
     494              :  *              Get the OID(s) of the standard hash support function(s) compatible with
     495              :  *              the given operator, operating on its LHS and/or RHS datatype as required.
     496              :  *
     497              :  * A function for the LHS type is sought and returned into *lhs_procno if
     498              :  * lhs_procno isn't NULL.  Similarly, a function for the RHS type is sought
     499              :  * and returned into *rhs_procno if rhs_procno isn't NULL.
     500              :  *
     501              :  * If the given operator is not cross-type, the results should be the same
     502              :  * function, but in cross-type situations they will be different.
     503              :  *
     504              :  * Returns true if able to find the requested function(s), false if not.
     505              :  * (This indicates that the operator should not have been marked oprcanhash.)
     506              :  */
     507              : bool
     508           18 : get_op_hash_functions(Oid opno,
     509              :                                           RegProcedure *lhs_procno, RegProcedure *rhs_procno)
     510              : {
     511           18 :         bool            result = false;
     512              :         CatCList   *catlist;
     513              :         int                     i;
     514              : 
     515              :         /* Ensure output args are initialized on failure */
     516           18 :         if (lhs_procno)
     517           18 :                 *lhs_procno = InvalidOid;
     518           18 :         if (rhs_procno)
     519           18 :                 *rhs_procno = InvalidOid;
     520              : 
     521              :         /*
     522              :          * Search pg_amop to see if the target operator is registered as the "="
     523              :          * operator of any hash opfamily.  If the operator is registered in
     524              :          * multiple opfamilies, assume we can use any one.
     525              :          */
     526           18 :         catlist = SearchSysCacheList1(AMOPOPID, ObjectIdGetDatum(opno));
     527              : 
     528           35 :         for (i = 0; i < catlist->n_members; i++)
     529              :         {
     530           35 :                 HeapTuple       tuple = &catlist->members[i]->tuple;
     531           35 :                 Form_pg_amop aform = (Form_pg_amop) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
     532              : 
     533           35 :                 if (aform->amopmethod == HASH_AM_OID &&
     534           18 :                         aform->amopstrategy == HTEqualStrategyNumber)
     535              :                 {
     536              :                         /*
     537              :                          * Get the matching support function(s).  Failure probably
     538              :                          * shouldn't happen --- it implies a bogus opfamily --- but
     539              :                          * continue looking if so.
     540              :                          */
     541           18 :                         if (lhs_procno)
     542              :                         {
     543           18 :                                 *lhs_procno = get_opfamily_proc(aform->amopfamily,
     544              :                                                                                                 aform->amoplefttype,
     545              :                                                                                                 aform->amoplefttype,
     546              :                                                                                                 HASHSTANDARD_PROC);
     547           18 :                                 if (!OidIsValid(*lhs_procno))
     548            0 :                                         continue;
     549              :                                 /* Matching LHS found, done if caller doesn't want RHS */
     550           18 :                                 if (!rhs_procno)
     551              :                                 {
     552            0 :                                         result = true;
     553            0 :                                         break;
     554              :                                 }
     555              :                                 /* Only one lookup needed if given operator is single-type */
     556           18 :                                 if (aform->amoplefttype == aform->amoprighttype)
     557              :                                 {
     558           18 :                                         *rhs_procno = *lhs_procno;
     559           18 :                                         result = true;
     560           18 :                                         break;
     561              :                                 }
     562              :                         }
     563            0 :                         if (rhs_procno)
     564              :                         {
     565            0 :                                 *rhs_procno = get_opfamily_proc(aform->amopfamily,
     566              :                                                                                                 aform->amoprighttype,
     567              :                                                                                                 aform->amoprighttype,
     568              :                                                                                                 HASHSTANDARD_PROC);
     569            0 :                                 if (!OidIsValid(*rhs_procno))
     570              :                                 {
     571              :                                         /* Forget any LHS function from this opfamily */
     572            0 :                                         if (lhs_procno)
     573            0 :                                                 *lhs_procno = InvalidOid;
     574            0 :                                         continue;
     575              :                                 }
     576              :                                 /* Matching RHS found, so done */
     577            0 :                                 result = true;
     578            0 :                                 break;
     579              :                         }
     580              :                 }
     581              :         }
     582              : 
     583           18 :         ReleaseSysCacheList(catlist);
     584              : 
     585           18 :         return result;
     586              : }
     587              : 
     588              : /*
     589              :  * get_op_btree_interpretation
     590              :  *              Given an operator's OID, find out which btree opfamilies it belongs to,
     591              :  *              and what properties it has within each one.  The results are returned
     592              :  *              as a palloc'd list of OpBtreeInterpretation structs.
     593              :  *
     594              :  * In addition to the normal btree operators, we consider a <> operator to be
     595              :  * a "member" of an opfamily if its negator is an equality operator of the
     596              :  * opfamily.  ROWCOMPARE_NE is returned as the strategy number for this case.
     597              :  */
     598              : List *
     599           20 : get_op_btree_interpretation(Oid opno)
     600              : {
     601           20 :         List       *result = NIL;
     602              :         OpBtreeInterpretation *thisresult;
     603              :         CatCList   *catlist;
     604              :         int                     i;
     605              : 
     606              :         /*
     607              :          * Find all the pg_amop entries containing the operator.
     608              :          */
     609           20 :         catlist = SearchSysCacheList1(AMOPOPID, ObjectIdGetDatum(opno));
     610              : 
     611           55 :         for (i = 0; i < catlist->n_members; i++)
     612              :         {
     613           35 :                 HeapTuple       op_tuple = &catlist->members[i]->tuple;
     614           35 :                 Form_pg_amop op_form = (Form_pg_amop) GETSTRUCT(op_tuple);
     615              :                 StrategyNumber op_strategy;
     616              : 
     617              :                 /* must be btree */
     618           35 :                 if (op_form->amopmethod != BTREE_AM_OID)
     619           22 :                         continue;
     620              : 
     621              :                 /* Get the operator's btree strategy number */
     622           13 :                 op_strategy = (StrategyNumber) op_form->amopstrategy;
     623              :                 Assert(op_strategy >= 1 && op_strategy <= 5);
     624              : 
     625              :                 thisresult = (OpBtreeInterpretation *)
     626           13 :                         palloc(sizeof(OpBtreeInterpretation));
     627           13 :                 thisresult->opfamily_id = op_form->amopfamily;
     628           13 :                 thisresult->strategy = op_strategy;
     629           13 :                 thisresult->oplefttype = op_form->amoplefttype;
     630           13 :                 thisresult->oprighttype = op_form->amoprighttype;
     631           13 :                 result = lappend(result, thisresult);
     632              :         }
     633              : 
     634           20 :         ReleaseSysCacheList(catlist);
     635              : 
     636              :         /*
     637              :          * If we didn't find any btree opfamily containing the operator, perhaps
     638              :          * it is a <> operator.  See if it has a negator that is in an opfamily.
     639              :          */
     640           20 :         if (result == NIL)
     641              :         {
     642            8 :                 Oid                     op_negator = get_negator(opno);
     643              : 
     644            8 :                 if (OidIsValid(op_negator))
     645              :                 {
     646            8 :                         catlist = SearchSysCacheList1(AMOPOPID,
     647              :                                                                                   ObjectIdGetDatum(op_negator));
     648              : 
     649           32 :                         for (i = 0; i < catlist->n_members; i++)
     650              :                         {
     651           24 :                                 HeapTuple       op_tuple = &catlist->members[i]->tuple;
     652           24 :                                 Form_pg_amop op_form = (Form_pg_amop) GETSTRUCT(op_tuple);
     653              :                                 StrategyNumber op_strategy;
     654              : 
     655              :                                 /* must be btree */
     656           24 :                                 if (op_form->amopmethod != BTREE_AM_OID)
     657           16 :                                         continue;
     658              : 
     659              :                                 /* Get the operator's btree strategy number */
     660            8 :                                 op_strategy = (StrategyNumber) op_form->amopstrategy;
     661              :                                 Assert(op_strategy >= 1 && op_strategy <= 5);
     662              : 
     663              :                                 /* Only consider negators that are = */
     664            8 :                                 if (op_strategy != BTEqualStrategyNumber)
     665            0 :                                         continue;
     666              : 
     667              :                                 /* OK, report it with "strategy" ROWCOMPARE_NE */
     668              :                                 thisresult = (OpBtreeInterpretation *)
     669            8 :                                         palloc(sizeof(OpBtreeInterpretation));
     670            8 :                                 thisresult->opfamily_id = op_form->amopfamily;
     671            8 :                                 thisresult->strategy = ROWCOMPARE_NE;
     672            8 :                                 thisresult->oplefttype = op_form->amoplefttype;
     673            8 :                                 thisresult->oprighttype = op_form->amoprighttype;
     674            8 :                                 result = lappend(result, thisresult);
     675              :                         }
     676              : 
     677            8 :                         ReleaseSysCacheList(catlist);
     678              :                 }
     679              :         }
     680              : 
     681           20 :         return result;
     682              : }
     683              : 
     684              : /*
     685              :  * equality_ops_are_compatible
     686              :  *              Return true if the two given equality operators have compatible
     687              :  *              semantics.
     688              :  *
     689              :  * This is trivially true if they are the same operator.  Otherwise,
     690              :  * we look to see if they can be found in the same btree or hash opfamily.
     691              :  * Either finding allows us to assume that they have compatible notions
     692              :  * of equality.  (The reason we need to do these pushups is that one might
     693              :  * be a cross-type operator; for instance int24eq vs int4eq.)
     694              :  */
     695              : bool
     696            0 : equality_ops_are_compatible(Oid opno1, Oid opno2)
     697              : {
     698              :         bool            result;
     699              :         CatCList   *catlist;
     700              :         int                     i;
     701              : 
     702              :         /* Easy if they're the same operator */
     703            0 :         if (opno1 == opno2)
     704            0 :                 return true;
     705              : 
     706              :         /*
     707              :          * We search through all the pg_amop entries for opno1.
     708              :          */
     709            0 :         catlist = SearchSysCacheList1(AMOPOPID, ObjectIdGetDatum(opno1));
     710              : 
     711            0 :         result = false;
     712            0 :         for (i = 0; i < catlist->n_members; i++)
     713              :         {
     714            0 :                 HeapTuple       op_tuple = &catlist->members[i]->tuple;
     715            0 :                 Form_pg_amop op_form = (Form_pg_amop) GETSTRUCT(op_tuple);
     716              : 
     717              :                 /* must be btree or hash */
     718            0 :                 if (op_form->amopmethod == BTREE_AM_OID ||
     719            0 :                         op_form->amopmethod == HASH_AM_OID)
     720              :                 {
     721            0 :                         if (op_in_opfamily(opno2, op_form->amopfamily))
     722              :                         {
     723            0 :                                 result = true;
     724            0 :                                 break;
     725              :                         }
     726              :                 }
     727              :         }
     728              : 
     729            0 :         ReleaseSysCacheList(catlist);
     730              : 
     731            0 :         return result;
     732              : }
     733              : 
     734              : /*
     735              :  * comparison_ops_are_compatible
     736              :  *              Return true if the two given comparison operators have compatible
     737              :  *              semantics.
     738              :  *
     739              :  * This is trivially true if they are the same operator.  Otherwise,
     740              :  * we look to see if they can be found in the same btree opfamily.
     741              :  * For example, '<' and '>=' ops match if they belong to the same family.
     742              :  *
     743              :  * (This is identical to equality_ops_are_compatible(), except that we
     744              :  * don't bother to examine hash opclasses.)
     745              :  */

     773           10 :                                 break;

     775              :                 }
     776              :         }
     777              :

     780           10 :         return result;

    1025              : /*

    1048              : /*                              ---------- COLLATION CACHE ----------                                    */
    1049              : 
    1050              : /*
    1051              :  * get_collation_name
    1052              :  *              Returns the name of a given pg_collation entry.
    1053              :  *
    1054              :  * Returns a palloc'd copy of the string, or NULL if no such collation.
    1055              :  *
    1056              :  * NOTE: since collation name is not unique, be wary of code that uses this
    1057              :  * for anything except preparing error messages.
    1058              :  */
    1059              : char *
    1060            0 : get_collation_name(Oid colloid)
    1061              : {
    1062              :         HeapTuple       tp;
    1063              : 
    1064            0 :         tp = SearchSysCache1(COLLOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(colloid));
    1065            0 :         if (HeapTupleIsValid(tp))

    1316              : /*
    1317              :  * op_mergejoinable
    1318              :  *
    1319              :  * Returns true if the operator is potentially mergejoinable.  (The planner
    1320              :  * will fail to find any mergejoin plans unless there are suitable btree
    1321              :  * opfamily entries for this operator and associated sortops.  The pg_operator
    1322              :  * flag is just a hint to tell the planner whether to bother looking.)
    1323              :  *
    1324              :  * In some cases (currently only array_eq and record_eq), mergejoinability
    1325              :  * depends on the specific input data type the operator is invoked for, so
    1326              :  * that must be passed as well. We currently assume that only one input's type
    1327              :  * is needed to check this --- by convention, pass the left input's data type.
    1328              :  */
    1329              : bool
    1330          224 : op_mergejoinable(Oid opno, Oid inputtype)
    1331              : {
    1332          224 :         bool            result = false;
    1333              :         HeapTuple       tp;
    1334              :         TypeCacheEntry *typentry;
    1335              : 
    1336              :         /*
    1337              :          * For array_eq or record_eq, we can sort if the element or field types
    1338              :          * are all sortable.  We could implement all the checks for that here, but
    1339              :          * the typcache already does that and caches the results too, so let's
    1340              :          * rely on the typcache.
    1341              :          */
    1342          224 :         if (opno == ARRAY_EQ_OP)
    1343              :         {
    1344            0 :                 typentry = lookup_type_cache(inputtype, TYPECACHE_CMP_PROC);
    1345            0 :                 if (typentry->cmp_proc == F_BTARRAYCMP)
    1346            0 :                         result = true;
    1347              :         }
    1348          224 :         else if (opno == RECORD_EQ_OP)
    1349              :         {
    1350            0 :                 typentry = lookup_type_cache(inputtype, TYPECACHE_CMP_PROC);
    1351            0 :                 if (typentry->cmp_proc == F_BTRECORDCMP)
    1352            0 :                         result = true;
    1353              :         }
    1354              :         else
    1355              :         {
    1356              :                 /* For all other operators, rely on pg_operator.oprcanmerge */
    1357          224 :                 tp = SearchSysCache1(OPEROID, ObjectIdGetDatum(opno));
    1358          224 :                 if (HeapTupleIsValid(tp))
    1359              :                 {
    1360          224 :                         Form_pg_operator optup = (Form_pg_operator) GETSTRUCT(tp);
    1361              : 
    1362          224 :                         result = optup->oprcanmerge;
    1363          224 :                         ReleaseSysCache(tp);
    1364              :                 }
    1365              :         }
    1366          224 :         return result;
    1367              : }
    1368              : 
    1369              : /*
    1370              :  * op_hashjoinable
    1371              :  *
    1372              :  * Returns true if the operator is hashjoinable.  (There must be a suitable
    1373              :  * hash opfamily entry for this operator if it is so marked.)

    1375              :  * In some cases (currently only array_eq), hashjoinability depends on the
    1376              :  * specific input data type the operator is invoked for, so that must be
    1377              :  * passed as well.  We currently assume that only one input's type is needed
    1378              :  * to check this --- by convention, pass the left input's data type.
    1379              :  */
    1380              : bool
    1381          122 : op_hashjoinable(Oid opno, Oid inputtype)

    1390              :         {
    1391            0 :                 typentry = lookup_type_cache(inputtype, TYPECACHE_HASH_PROC);
    1392            0 :                 if (typentry->hash_proc == F_HASH_ARRAY)

    1394              :         }

    1396              :         {

    1399          122 :                 if (HeapTupleIsValid(tp))
    1400              :                 {

    1402              :

    2155              : /*

    2161              :  *              many places need both.  Might as well get them with one cache lookup

    2163              :  *              returning a bogus value when given a bad type OID.
    2164              :  */

    2174          225 :         typtup = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tp);

    2176          225 :         *typbyval = typtup->typbyval;
    2177          225 :         ReleaseSysCache(tp);
    2178          225 : }

    2201              : 
    2202              : /*
    2203              :  * getTypeIOParam
    2204              :  *              Given a pg_type row, select the type OID to pass to I/O functions
    2205              :  *
    2206              :  * Formerly, all I/O functions were passed pg_type.typelem as their second
    2207              :  * parameter, but we now have a more complex rule about what to pass.
    2208              :  * This knowledge is intended to be centralized here --- direct references
    2209              :  * to typelem elsewhere in the code are wrong, if they are associated with
    2210              :  * I/O calls and not with actual subscripting operations!  (But see
    2211              :  * bootstrap.c's boot_get_type_io_data() if you need to change this.)
    2212              :  *
    2213              :  * As of PostgreSQL 8.1, output functions receive only the value itself
    2214              :  * and not any auxiliary parameters, so the name of this routine is now
    2215              :  * a bit of a misnomer ... it should be getTypeInputParam.
    2216              :  */
    2217              : Oid
    2218         1411 : getTypeIOParam(HeapTuple typeTuple)
    2219              : {
    2220         1411 :         Form_pg_type typeStruct = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(typeTuple);
    2221              : 
    2222              :         /*
    2223              :          * Array types get their typelem as parameter; everybody else gets their
    2224              :          * own type OID as parameter.
    2225              :          */
    2226         1411 :         if (OidIsValid(typeStruct->typelem))
    2227          204 :                 return typeStruct->typelem;
    2228              :         else
    2229         1207 :                 return typeStruct->oid;
    2230              : }
    2231              :

    2352              : /*
    2353              :  * get_typdefault

    2443              : /*
    2444              :  * getBaseTypeAndTypmod
    2445              :  *              If the given type is a domain, return its base type and typmod;
    2446              :  *              otherwise return the type's own OID, and leave *typmod unchanged.
    2447              :  *
    2448              :  * Note that the "applied typmod" should be -1 for every domain level
    2449              :  * above the bottommost; therefore, if the passed-in typid is indeed
    2450              :  * a domain, *typmod should be -1.
    2451              :  */

    2453        12188 : getBaseTypeAndTypmod(Oid typid, int32 *typmod)
    2454              : {

    2459         2778 :         {
    2460              :                 HeapTuple       tup;
    2461              :                 Form_pg_type typTup;

    2467        14966 :                 if (typTup->typtype != TYPTYPE_DOMAIN)
    2468              :                 {

    2470        12188 :                         ReleaseSysCache(tup);
    2471        12188 :                         break;
    2472              :                 }
    2473              : 
    2474              :                 Assert(*typmod == -1);
    2475         2778 :                 typid = typTup->typbasetype;
    2476         2778 :                 *typmod = typTup->typtypmod;
    2477              : 
    2478         2778 :                 ReleaseSysCache(tup);
    2479              :         }
    2480              : 
    2481        12188 :         return typid;
    2482              : }

    2484              : /*
    2485              :  * get_typavgwidth
    2486              :  *
    2487              :  *        Given a type OID and a typmod value (pass -1 if typmod is unknown),
    2488              :  *        estimate the average width of values of the type.  This is used by
    2489              :  *        the planner, which doesn't require absolutely correct results;
    2490              :  *        it's OK (and expected) to guess if we don't know for sure.
    2491              :  */

    2493         1545 : get_typavgwidth(Oid typid, int32 typmod)
    2494              : {
    2495         1545 :         int                     typlen = get_typlen(typid);
    2496              :         int32           maxwidth;
    2497              : 
    2498              :         /*
    2499              :          * Easy if it's a fixed-width type
    2500              :          */
    2501         1545 :         if (typlen > 0)
    2502         1179 :                 return typlen;
    2503              : 
    2504              :         /*
    2505              :          * type_maximum_size knows the encoding of typmod for some datatypes;
    2506              :          * don't duplicate that knowledge here.
    2507              :          */
    2508          366 :         maxwidth = type_maximum_size(typid, typmod);
    2509          366 :         if (maxwidth > 0)
    2510              :         {
    2511              :                 /*
    2512              :                  * For BPCHAR, the max width is also the only width.  Otherwise we
    2513              :                  * need to guess about the typical data width given the max. A sliding
    2514              :                  * scale for percentage of max width seems reasonable.
    2515              :                  */
    2516            0 :                 if (typid == BPCHAROID)
    2517            0 :                         return maxwidth;
    2518            0 :                 if (maxwidth <= 32)
    2519            0 :                         return maxwidth;        /* assume full width */
    2520            0 :                 if (maxwidth < 1000)
    2521            0 :                         return 32 + (maxwidth - 32) / 2;        /* assume 50% */
    2522              : 
    2523              :                 /*
    2524              :                  * Beyond 1000, assume we're looking at something like
    2525              :                  * "varchar(10000)" where the limit isn't actually reached often, and
    2526              :                  * use a fixed estimate.
    2527              :                  */
    2528            0 :                 return 32 + (1000 - 32) / 2;
    2529              :         }
    2530              : 
    2531              :         /*
    2532              :          * Oops, we have no idea ... wild guess time.
    2533              :          */
    2534          366 :         return 32;
    2535              : }

    2584              :         }
    2585           71 :         return false;

    2993              : /*

    3000              :  * trees, so we don't need an "inh" parameter.

    3031              : 
    3032              : /*
    3033              :  * get_attstatsslot
    3034              :  *
    3035              :  *              Extract the contents of a "slot" of a pg_statistic tuple.
    3036              :  *              Returns true if requested slot type was found, else false.
    3037              :  *
    3038              :  * Unlike other routines in this file, this takes a pointer to an
    3039              :  * already-looked-up tuple in the pg_statistic cache.  We do this since
    3040              :  * most callers will want to extract more than one value from the cache
    3041              :  * entry, and we don't want to repeat the cache lookup unnecessarily.
    3042              :  * Also, this API allows this routine to be used with statistics tuples
    3043              :  * that have been provided by a stats hook and didn't really come from
    3044              :  * pg_statistic.
    3045              :  *
    3046              :  * sslot: pointer to output area (typically, a local variable in the caller).
    3047              :  * statstuple: pg_statistic tuple to be examined.
    3048              :  * reqkind: STAKIND code for desired statistics slot kind.
    3049              :  * reqop: STAOP value wanted, or InvalidOid if don't care.
    3050              :  * flags: bitmask of ATTSTATSSLOT_VALUES and/or ATTSTATSSLOT_NUMBERS.
    3051              :  *
    3052              :  * If a matching slot is found, true is returned, and *sslot is filled thus:
    3053              :  * staop: receives the actual STAOP value.
    3054              :  * stacoll: receives the actual STACOLL value.
    3055              :  * valuetype: receives actual datatype of the elements of stavalues.
    3056              :  * values: receives pointer to an array of the slot's stavalues.
    3057              :  * nvalues: receives number of stavalues.
    3058              :  * numbers: receives pointer to an array of the slot's stanumbers (as float4).
    3059              :  * nnumbers: receives number of stanumbers.
    3060              :  *
    3061              :  * valuetype/values/nvalues are InvalidOid/NULL/0 if ATTSTATSSLOT_VALUES
    3062              :  * wasn't specified.  Likewise, numbers/nnumbers are NULL/0 if
    3063              :  * ATTSTATSSLOT_NUMBERS wasn't specified.
    3064              :  *
    3065              :  * If no matching slot is found, false is returned, and *sslot is zeroed.
    3066              :  *
    3067              :  * Note that the current API doesn't allow for searching for a slot with
    3068              :  * a particular collation.  If we ever actually support recording more than
    3069              :  * one collation, we'll have to extend the API, but for now simple is good.
    3070              :  *
    3071              :  * The data referred to by the fields of sslot is locally palloc'd and
    3072              :  * is independent of the original pg_statistic tuple.  When the caller
    3073              :  * is done with it, call free_attstatsslot to release the palloc'd data.
    3074              :  *
    3075              :  * If it's desirable to call free_attstatsslot when get_attstatsslot might
    3076              :  * not have been called, memset'ing sslot to zeroes will allow that.
    3077              :  */
    3078              : bool
    3079          208 : get_attstatsslot(AttStatsSlot *sslot, HeapTuple statstuple,
    3080              :                                  int reqkind, Oid reqop, int flags)
    3081              : {
    3082          208 :         Form_pg_statistic stats = (Form_pg_statistic) GETSTRUCT(statstuple);
    3083              :         int                     i;
    3084              :         Datum           val;
    3085              :         bool            isnull;
    3086              :         ArrayType  *statarray;
    3087              :         Oid                     arrayelemtype;
    3088              :         int                     narrayelem;
    3089              :         HeapTuple       typeTuple;
    3090              :         Form_pg_type typeForm;
    3091              : 
    3092              :         /* initialize *sslot properly */
    3093          208 :         memset(sslot, 0, sizeof(AttStatsSlot));
    3094              : 
    3095          613 :         for (i = 0; i < STATISTIC_NUM_SLOTS; i++)
    3096              :         {
    3097          549 :                 if ((&stats->stakind1)[i] == reqkind &&
    3098           71 :                         (reqop == InvalidOid || (&stats->staop1)[i] == reqop))
    3099              :                         break;
    3100              :         }
    3101          208 :         if (i >= STATISTIC_NUM_SLOTS)
    3102           64 :                 return false;                   /* not there */
    3103              : 
    3104          144 :         sslot->staop = (&stats->staop1)[i];
    3105          144 :         sslot->stacoll = (&stats->stacoll1)[i];
    3106              : 
    3107          144 :         if (flags & ATTSTATSSLOT_VALUES)
    3108              :         {
    3109           66 :                 val = SysCacheGetAttr(STATRELATTINH, statstuple,
    3110           66 :                                                           Anum_pg_statistic_stavalues1 + i,
    3111              :                                                           &isnull);
    3112           66 :                 if (isnull)
    3113            0 :                         elog(ERROR, "stavalues is null");
    3114              : 
    3115              :                 /*
    3116              :                  * Detoast the array if needed, and in any case make a copy that's
    3117              :                  * under control of this AttStatsSlot.
    3118              :                  */
    3119           66 :                 statarray = DatumGetArrayTypePCopy(val);
    3120              : 
    3121              :                 /*
    3122              :                  * Extract the actual array element type, and pass it back in case the
    3123              :                  * caller needs it.
    3124              :                  */
    3125           66 :                 sslot->valuetype = arrayelemtype = ARR_ELEMTYPE(statarray);
    3126              : 
    3127              :                 /* Need info about element type */
    3128           66 :                 typeTuple = SearchSysCache1(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(arrayelemtype));
    3129           66 :                 if (!HeapTupleIsValid(typeTuple))
    3130            0 :                         elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", arrayelemtype);
    3131           66 :                 typeForm = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(typeTuple);
    3132              : 
    3133              :                 /* Deconstruct array into Datum elements; NULLs not expected */
    3134           66 :                 deconstruct_array(statarray,
    3135              :                                                   arrayelemtype,
    3136           66 :                                                   typeForm->typlen,
    3137           66 :                                                   typeForm->typbyval,
    3138           66 :                                                   typeForm->typalign,
    3139              :                                                   &sslot->values, NULL, &sslot->nvalues);
    3140              : 
    3141              :                 /*
    3142              :                  * If the element type is pass-by-reference, we now have a bunch of
    3143              :                  * Datums that are pointers into the statarray, so we need to keep
    3144              :                  * that until free_attstatsslot.  Otherwise, all the useful info is in
    3145              :                  * sslot->values[], so we can free the array object immediately.
    3146              :                  */
    3147           66 :                 if (!typeForm->typbyval)
    3148           11 :                         sslot->values_arr = statarray;
    3149              :                 else
    3150           55 :                         pfree(statarray);
    3151              : 
    3152           66 :                 ReleaseSysCache(typeTuple);
    3153              :         }
    3154              : 
    3155          144 :         if (flags & ATTSTATSSLOT_NUMBERS)
    3156              :         {
    3157          125 :                 val = SysCacheGetAttr(STATRELATTINH, statstuple,
    3158          125 :                                                           Anum_pg_statistic_stanumbers1 + i,
    3159              :                                                           &isnull);
    3160          125 :                 if (isnull)
    3161            0 :                         elog(ERROR, "stanumbers is null");
    3162              : 
    3163              :                 /*
    3164              :                  * Detoast the array if needed, and in any case make a copy that's
    3165              :                  * under control of this AttStatsSlot.
    3166              :                  */
    3167          125 :                 statarray = DatumGetArrayTypePCopy(val);
    3168              : 
    3169              :                 /*
    3170              :                  * We expect the array to be a 1-D float4 array; verify that. We don't
    3171              :                  * need to use deconstruct_array() since the array data is just going
    3172              :                  * to look like a C array of float4 values.
    3173              :                  */
    3174          125 :                 narrayelem = ARR_DIMS(statarray)[0];
    3175          125 :                 if (ARR_NDIM(statarray) != 1 || narrayelem <= 0 ||
    3176          125 :                         ARR_HASNULL(statarray) ||
    3177          125 :                         ARR_ELEMTYPE(statarray) != FLOAT4OID)
    3178            0 :                         elog(ERROR, "stanumbers is not a 1-D float4 array");
    3179              : 
    3180              :                 /* Give caller a pointer directly into the statarray */
    3181          125 :                 sslot->numbers = (float4 *) ARR_DATA_PTR(statarray);
    3182          125 :                 sslot->nnumbers = narrayelem;
    3183              : 
    3184              :                 /* We'll free the statarray in free_attstatsslot */
    3185          125 :                 sslot->numbers_arr = statarray;
    3186              :         }
    3187              : 
    3188          144 :         return true;
    3189              : }
    3190              : 
    3191              : /*
    3192              :  * free_attstatsslot
    3193              :  *              Free data allocated by get_attstatsslot
    3194              :  */
    3195              : void
    3196          296 : free_attstatsslot(AttStatsSlot *sslot)
    3197              : {
    3198              :         /* The values[] array was separately palloc'd by deconstruct_array */
    3199          296 :         if (sslot->values)
    3200           66 :                 pfree(sslot->values);
    3201              :         /* The numbers[] array points into numbers_arr, do not pfree it */
    3202              :         /* Free the detoasted array objects, if any */
    3203          296 :         if (sslot->values_arr)
    3204           11 :                 pfree(sslot->values_arr);
    3205          296 :         if (sslot->numbers_arr)
    3206          125 :                 pfree(sslot->numbers_arr);
    3207          296 : }

    3211              : /*
    3212              :  * get_namespace_name

    3214              :  *
    3215              :  * Returns a palloc'd copy of the string, or NULL if no such namespace.
    3216              :  */
    3217              : char *
    3218         1242 : get_namespace_name(Oid nspid)

    3220              :         HeapTuple       tp;

    3222         1242 :         tp = SearchSysCache1(NAMESPACEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(nspid));

    3226              :                 char       *result;
    3227              : 
    3228         1242 :                 result = pstrdup(NameStr(nsptup->nspname));
    3229         1242 :                 ReleaseSysCache(tp);
    3230         1242 :                 return result;
    3231              :         }
    3232              :         else
    3233            0 :                 return NULL;
    3234              : }
    3235              : 
    3236              : /*
    3237              :  * get_namespace_name_or_temp
    3238              :  *              As above, but if it is this backend's temporary namespace, return

    3241              : char *
    3242           46 : get_namespace_name_or_temp(Oid nspid)
    3243              : {
    3244           46 :         if (isTempNamespace(nspid))
    3245            0 :                 return "pg_temp";
    3246              :         else
    3247           46 :                 return get_namespace_name(nspid);
